question.text: Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, which of the following entities is PROHIBITED from making any open offer (including competing offers)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SAST 2011, fugitive economic offenders cannot make any offer at all — they are completely barred. This is a stricter restriction than what applies to wilful defaulters. **The Trap:** Wilful defaulters cannot make open offers but CAN make competing offers. Fugitive economic offenders face a total ban. This distinction is commonly tested. **Cross-Exam Context:** The Fugitive Economic Offenders Act 2018 intersects with multiple NISM certifications including Series IX and Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always run a defaulter/fugitive check on the acquirer before structuring any open offer. Discovering this disqualification mid-transaction can be catastrophic and reputation-destroying.
